name: Amish Friendship Bread
recipeInstructions:
1: Mix all the starter ingredients well (1 cup flour, 1 cup warm water, 1/2 cup sugar, and 1 package yeast) in a medium-sized glass bowl. Cover with a dinner plate so as not to cover tightly. Let stand overnight in a warm place. Stir down each day for 4 days. Do not refrigerate batter at any time.
2: On the fifth day, add 1 cup flour, 1 cup milk, and 1/2 cup sugar to the starter. Stir well and let it rest until the next day.
3: Continue adding ingredients daily on the sixth through tenth days:
- Day 6: Add 1 cup flour, 1 cup milk, and 1/2 cup sugar.
- Day 7: Add 1 cup flour, 1 cup milk, and 1/2 cup sugar.
- Day 8: Add 1 cup flour, 1 cup milk, and 1/2 cup sugar.
- Day 9: Add 1 cup flour, 1 cup milk, and 1/2 cup sugar.
4: On the tenth day, divide the starter into three equal parts. Give one part to each of your friends along with a copy of these instructions.
5: Use the remaining dough to bake your favorite bread or prepare streusel as follows:
Batter: Mix together (in a separate bowl) 2/3 cup cooking oil, 1 cup sugar, 2 cups flour, 2 teaspoons baking soda, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and 3 eggs.
Streusel: In another bowl, combine 1/3 cup melted margarine, 1/2 cup white sugar, 1/2 cup brown sugar, 1/2 cup quick oats, and (optional) 1 cup nuts.
6. Pour half of the batter into a greased and floured 9 x 13 baking pan. Sprinkle half of the streusel mixture over the batter. Cover with the remaining batter and streusel.
7. Bake at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 35-40 minutes.
Source: Lancaster County Cookbook
